title: Porous Navier-Stokes Configuration
description: Configuration schema for the Gridap-based Porous Navier-Stokes solver.

physical_properties.title: Physical Properties
physical_properties.description: Physical properties of the fluid and the porous medium, including numerical floor values and reaction terms.

f_x.description: Body force component in the x-direction (e.g., gravity).

f_y.description: Body force component in the y-direction.

eps_val.description: Absolute numerical stabilization limit for pressure.

sigma_constant.description: Constant term parameter for the reaction model.

sigma_linear.description: Linear drag coefficient (A) derived from Darcy's law.

sigma_nonlinear.description: Nonlinear drag coefficient (B) from Forchheimer correction.

u_base_floor_ref.description: Reference base velocity for regularization floor.

epsilon_floor.description: Absolute velocity magnitude cutoff to prevent divide-by-zero.

tau_regularization_limit.description: Minimum limit applied to stabilization parameters (tau).

domain.title: Domain Configuration
domain.description: Geometrical description and dimensions of the domain and the spatial distribution of the porous medium.

r_1.description: Inner radius geometry control for porosity field.

r_2.description: Outer radius geometry control for porosity field.

bounding_box.description: Bounding box lengths for the geometry [xmin, xmax, ymin, ymax].

numerical_method.title: Numerical Method
numerical_method.description: Discretization, stabilization, mesh parsing, and nonlinear solver components.

element_spaces.title: Element Spaces
element_spaces.description: Finite element type and order degrees.

equal_order_only.description: Flag used primarily in testing to enforce equal-order elements.

stabilization.title: Stabilization Configuration
stabilization.description: Mechanisms for stabilizing the Galerkin solver.

solver.title: Solver Configuration
solver.description: Hyperparameters controlling the Picard and Exact Newton linear/nonlinear solvers.

picard_iterations.description: Initial number of Picard iterations before switching to Newton.

dynamic_picard_re_threshold.description: Re threshold above which Picard's iteration budget is widened to dynamic_picard_re_iterations.

dynamic_picard_re_iterations.description: Picard iteration budget used at high Re (>= dynamic_picard_re_threshold).

dynamic_picard_da_threshold.description: Da threshold above which Picard's iteration budget is widened to dynamic_picard_da_iterations.

dynamic_picard_da_iterations.description: Picard iteration budget used at high Da (>= dynamic_picard_da_threshold).

dynamic_newton_re_threshold.description: Re threshold above which Newton's iteration budget is widened to dynamic_newton_re_iterations (Phase 6 §2.1 stiff-cell fix).

dynamic_newton_re_iterations.description: Newton iteration budget used at high Re (>= dynamic_newton_re_threshold). Defaults to 60 to cover both the smoothing-out phase and the quadratic tail.

xtol.description: Relative norm tolerance for the solution increment (dx).

picard_handoff_ftol.description: Residual tolerance for the Picard smoother to declare 'good enough' and hand off to Newton. Should be looser than ftol since Picard is a globalization aid, not a precise solver. Must be >= ftol.

dynamic_ftol_ceiling.description: Upper bound on the dynamically-tightened ftol that couples residual tolerance to mesh resolution (O(h^(kv+1))). Prevents oversolving relative to discretization error.

dynamic_ftol_spatial_safety_factor.description: Safety factor in (0, 1] applied to the spatially-scaled ftol. Lower values demand tighter convergence relative to discretization error.

eps_tol_momentum.description: Scale-free momentum tolerance tol_M: the nonlinear solve is converged when eps_M = ||r_M||/D_M <= tol_M, where D_M is the dynamic force-magnitude envelope (convergence_criterion.jl). Segment-independent (no per-segment re-anchoring). eps_M -> 0 at the discrete solution.

eps_tol_mass.description: Scale-free mass tolerance tol_C: converged when eps_C = ||div(alpha u)||/||grad(alpha u)|| <= tol_C. eps_C floors at O(h^kv) (the discrete-divergence floor) and is bounded by sqrt(d); set tol_C above that floor.

max_increases.description: Number of successive residual increases before divergence abort.

freeze_jacobian_cusp.description: If true, freezes the magnitude of velocity in the nonlinearity Jacobian cross-terms.

linesearch_alpha_min.description: Minimum step size allowed before linesearch fails.

armijo_c1.description: Armijo sufficient decrease parameter (c1) for the linesearch.

divergence_merit_factor.description: Factor by which the merit function is allowed to increase before counting as a divergence step.

stagnation_noise_floor.description: The threshold for the residual inf-norm below which continuous divergence stops safely rather than crashing the solver.

condition_noise_floor_baseline.description: Baseline coefficient for the condition-aware noise floor (multiplies a system-conditioning estimate to produce the effective floor).

condition_noise_floor_absolute_min.description: Hard lower bound for the condition-aware noise floor (prevents it from collapsing to numerical zero).

condition_noise_floor_safety_factor.description: Safety factor (>= 1) applied to the condition-aware noise floor before use.

noise_floor_success_max_ftol_multiple.description: [honest-exit] A noise-floor solver stop is reported as CONVERGED only if the final residual inf-norm is <= this multiple of the (dynamic) ftol. Must be >= 1. Use a very large value (e.g. 1e30) to disable the gate (legacy behaviour: any noise-floor stop counts as success); use a finite value (e.g. 10.0) to reject high-Re fold stalls where the residual sits ~10^2-10^3x above ftol from masquerading as a true root.

max_linesearch_iterations.description: The strict upper limit mapped structurally dynamically onto exact armijo linesearch cycles avoiding topological float exhaustion.

linesearch_contraction_factor.description: Linesearch scalar parameter tracking geometric bounds across inner iterations recursively bounding unconstrained domains. (alpha *= factor).

run_diagnostics.description: Enable expanded diagnostics / debug print statements.

ablation_mode.description: Switch behavior of specific solver terms for systematic testing. 'picard_only' forces the Picard/Oseen Jacobian for every nonlinear stage (diagnostic: tests whether a converged result is solver-independent).

experimental_reaction_mode.description: Toggles advanced reaction handling behaviors for high-Re modes.

newton_stall_window.description: [iterator-scheduling P1] Consecutive non-improving Newton iterations before bailing to the cascade's Picard stage (stop_reason 'no_progress_stall'). 0 disables the guard (default; Newton runs its full budget = today's behaviour). Use >= 2 so a one-step plateau before quadratic descent is not bailed.

newton_stall_min_rel_improvement.description: [iterator-scheduling P1] Relative ||R||_inf reduction that counts as 'progress' for the stall window. In [0, 1). Keep small (e.g. 1e-3) so genuine quadratic descent always resets the window.

pingpong_enabled.description: [iterator-scheduling P4] Master switch for the adaptive Newton<->Picard ping-pong cascade. false (default) keeps the one-way Newton->Picard->Newton cascade (today's behaviour).

pingpong_max_swaps.description: [iterator-scheduling P4] Hard cap on Newton<->Picard alternations. 0 (with pingpong_enabled=false) is inert. Validate >= 0.

pingpong_picard_gain_orders.description: [iterator-scheduling P4] Orders of magnitude ||R||_inf must drop during a Picard segment before handing back to Newton (||R||_inf <= ||R0||_inf * 10^-gain). Read only when pingpong_enabled=true. Validate > 0.

newton_residual_divergence_patience.description: [divergence guard] Consecutive Newton iterations whose residual ||R||_inf rises beyond divergence_merit_factor before the Newton solve aborts and the cascade hands off to Picard. The :newton divergence safeguard otherwise watches the merit Phi, which the Armijo line search forces down, so a residual-divergent solve runs its full iteration budget. 0 (default) disables this guard (legacy). Validate >= 0.

partition.description: Number of elements in each direction [nx, ny].

convergence_partitions.description: Set of partitions for convergence studies (e.g., MMS tests).

output.title: Output Configuration
output.description: Path and naming for saving results.

basename.description: Prefix applied to saved solution files.

max_n_pert.description: Maximum noise permutations for MMS execution config.

epsilon_pert.description: Epsilon perturbations scale for condition tests.

h5_filename.description: Filename of H5 results database used in manufactured solutions.
